duplicated_keys.computed_default: os.environ.get("PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL", _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL) — the module-local const _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2" is REDEFINED IDENTICALLY in both modules (entailment_judge.py:107 and semantic_conflict_detector.py:80). Real cross-module duplication of both the default value/const and the read. A drift in one const silently diverges the entailment model across the two NLI paths.

result.baseline: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors in 37.60s (unchanged from documented phase0/phase1 baseline of 11 pre-existing collection errors). Command: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q. Worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase-acc on branch chore/review-readiness-acc (from chore/review-readiness-phase1); phase0 oracle harness (*.py + 3 acceptance cassettes) force-copied in. single_site_keys_count: ~48 — the AST scan of every os.getenv/os.environ.get read with a genuinely computed (non-literal) default found ~51 such keys total; exactly 3 (below) are read at 2+ call sites, the remaining ~48 are read at a single site and are NOT worth an accessor per codex's proven-duplication rule. NOTE: the many int()/float()-coerced keys with a plain STRING-LITERAL default (e.g. int(os.getenv(K,\"16384\"))) were excluded from the computed-default category: identical-literal ones are already registry-eligible via resolve(), and divergent-literal ones (PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY, PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION, PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD, etc.) are the 20 already-tracked conflicting-default keys in config_conflicts.md, out of scope. Recommendation: a surgical 3-accessor change (typed getters that own each computed default once), NOT a blanket 46-key migration.

result.note: Added 3 typed computed-default accessors to /home/polaris/wt/phase-acc/src/polaris_graph/settings.py: get_entailment_model() -> str (os.environ.get("PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL","z-ai/glm-5.2")), get_max_docling_pdf_bytes() -> int (int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES", str(5*1024*1024)))), get_v6_redis_url() -> str (os.environ.get("POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL","redis://localhost:6379/0")). Each does a fresh os.getenv read on every call, preserving env>default precedence and coercion. Replaced 6 call sites: entailment_judge.py:663, semantic_conflict_detector.py:692 (both via `from src.polaris_graph.settings import get_entailment_model`), access_bypass.py:5781 and :5950 (lazy import of get_max_docling_pdf_bytes; PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_PAGES left untouched as a single-site key), broker.py:76 and run_events.py:29 (lazy import of get_v6_redis_url). Byte-identical parity verified with a harness: unset->documented defaults, set->override wins, int() returns int, and a non-int PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES still raises ValueError exactly as the inline int() did (the two access_bypass sites already wrap/guard these). Deliberately did NOT delete the now-orphaned module-local consts: entailment_judge._DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL is imported by strict_verify.py and credibility_pass.py and asserted by tests/polaris_graph/test_f3_judge_availability_canary.py and test_entailment_judge_rate_limit_backoff_u16.py; broker.DEFAULT_REDIS_URL is imported+asserted by tests/v6/test_broker.py. Removing them is out of scope for "replace the call sites" and would break collection. semantic_conflict_detector._DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL and run_events.DEFAULT_REDIS_URL are now unreferenced but left in place (harmless, out of scope). py_compile passed on all 6 touched files; all 6 modules import cleanly. Collection: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (15.27s) — count and the 11 pre-existing error files (mesh/playwright/auth/hle/clarification, none in the modified modules) unchanged from the phase0/phase1 baseline; only wall-clock time differs (non-deterministic).
